meta.title: Thesis
meta.description: Bachelor thesis by Alejandro Innocenzi and Matteo Vittori on SEF, a modular Python framework for video stream analysis.

hero.coverAlt: Cover of the SEF bachelor thesis
hero.title: Design and development of a modular framework for video-stream analysis in Python.

abstract.title: A framework for change, not another vision algorithm.
abstract.lead: Video-analysis pipelines often grow as monolithic scripts that couple acquisition, processing, signal extraction, metrics, and visualisation. That makes experiments difficult to extend, test, and reproduce.
abstract.body: The thesis presents SEF, a modular Python framework that composes explicit, replaceable components around a stable core. Specialised tools such as OpenCV, Matplotlib, YOLO Pose, and Streamlit remain peripheral adapters, while the runtime coordinates batch and streaming execution, typed artifacts, events, configuration, and multiple user interfaces.

contributions.title: Architecture made concrete.
items: A layered architecture with an independent core and specialised adapters.
Explicit contracts for extraction, processing, cleaning, analysis, visualisation, and export.
A component registry and plugin model that extends the system without changing its core.
One runtime model spanning Python API, declarative configuration, CLI, and SEF Studio.
Batch, streaming, hybrid materialisation, events, and UI-agnostic outputs.

chapters.title: Seven steps from problem to evidence.

items.title: Introduction
items.description: Motivation, objectives, approach, contribution, and methodology.

items.title: Problem & requirements
items.description: The video-to-signal model, use cases, and architectural constraints.

items.title: Framework architecture
items.description: Core and adapters, contracts, registry, runtime, events, and SEF Studio.

items.title: Technology
items.description: Computer-vision libraries, interface tools, quality, and documentation.

items.title: Use cases
items.description: Tracking, optical flow, motion magnification, ArUco, and pose estimation.

items.title: Benchmarks
items.description: Batch and streaming performance, policies, overhead, and memory scalability.

items.title: Discussion
items.description: Validity, project maturity, limitations, and future development.

sef.title: The research and the software are one argument.
sef.body: The thesis identifies the structural problem, derives requirements, defines the architecture, and evaluates its consequences. SEF is the executable form of that argument: contracts, plugins, runtime planning, events, outputs, and working use cases.
